questionText: A big-wheel car's height above the ground is modelled by $h(t) = 8\sin(30t)^\circ + 15\cos(30t)^\circ + 20$ metres, where $t$ is in minutes. Using an R-form for $8\sin x + 15\cos x$, find the maximum height and the smallest positive $t$ at which it first occurs.

steps.description: Set up the R-form for the varying part
steps.explanation: Combining the two oscillating terms into one sine reveals the amplitude and hence the maximum height.
The amplitude is the root of the sum of squares (an $8$-$15$-$17$ triangle).
The ratio gives the phase angle.
Substituting the R-form gives a single-sine model with a vertical shift of $20$.
The maximum occurs when the sine equals $1$, adding the amplitude to the offset.

steps.description: Set the sine to its peak
steps.explanation: The first maximum occurs when the argument first equals $90^\circ$.
Dividing by $30$ gives the smallest positive time, about $0.94$ minutes.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: Maximum height $37$ m, first at $t \approx 0.94$ minutes.
